reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to remediate PAM password minlen. Prior attempt edited /etc/security/pwquality.conf directly, but scan still fails. Maybe the system uses /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf overrides. We should check which files exist. Plan: read /etc/security/pwquality.conf and any /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf. Determine if minlen present. Then ensure only one minlen setting, e.g., set in /etc/security/pwquality.conf. Use backup with sed -i.bak. Use authselect to ensure pam_pwquality module is used with correct option? The rule likely just checks that minlen is set in the pwquality config. Maybe the file is not being read because pam_pwquality is configured with pam_pwquality.so enforce_for_root. The setting is read from /etc/security/pwquality.conf (default) unless overridden. The previous edit may have failed because the line was not present or comment format different, but sed changed. However scan still fails. Perhaps file requires a newline at end? Or they expect "minlen = 8" with spaces exactly? We should ensure the line is present and no duplicate in .conf.d. So plan: backup pwquality.conf .bak, then ensure any minlen lines in pwquality.conf.d are removed. Use find to list files; use read_file to see list. Then for each .conf file we can use sed -i.bak '/^\s*#? *minlen\s*=/d' to delete lines, then add minlen = 8 to pwquality.conf if not present.

But we cannot use loops in single command? We can issue multiple run_cmd sequentially. That's fine.

We need to follow workflow: first provide PLAN, then call review_plan. So we should output plan description and then request review.
